chapter_title.ar: باب الْقَوْلِ عِنْدَ دُخُولِ الْخَلَاءِ وَ عِنْدَ الْخُرُوجِ وَ الِاسْتِنْجَاءِ وَ مَنْ نَسِيَهُ وَ التَّسْمِيَةِ عِنْدَ الدُّخُولِ وَ عِنْدَ الْوُضُوءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 12 – The words (to be spoken) during entering the toilet, and during the exit, and the cleansing (afterwards), and the one who forgets, and Naming (Bismillah) during the entering and during the Ablution (Wudhu)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/55)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/55)

parts.text: عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَبْدِ رَبِّهِ

text: قُلْتُ لَهُ مَا تَقُولُ فِي الْفَصِّ يُتَّخَذُ مِنْ حِجَارَةِ زُمُرُّدٍ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ وَ لَكِنْ إِذَا أَرَادَ الِاسْتِنْجَاءَ نَزَعَهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ali Bin Al Husayn Bin Abd Rabbih who said,

‘I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘What are you saying regarding ring stone taken from the emerald stones’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem with it, but whenever one intends to cleanse himself, he should remove it (beforehand)’.
en.sarwar: 6. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn Ahmad from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn ‘Abd Rabbihi who has said the following: “I once asked him (the Imam), ‘Is it permissible to wear a ring with a stone of Emerald?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘It is not harmful but during washing one’s self after using the toilet, one should remove it.’”
